title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
In the restaurant of the Brawnsham Hotel, Lazari introduces a new employee, Thomas Breenell. Breenell testifies that he met Richard Nigg at 7:30 p.m. near the dining room elevators. Nigg expressed concern for Mrs. Westphal and Miss Gransbury, asking Breenell to ensure their dinner bill was not charged to their accounts. Breenell notes that Nigg had just received a meal in Room 317 fifteen minutes prior, raising questions about why Nigg left his food to handle the bill.

Mr. Burbank addresses the crowd, questioning Nigg's movements and motives. A woman named Theresa Studd becomes distressed by the tension, but is calmed by a young woman and a promise from the narrator that she may return to her children soon. Poirot then presents a white piece of paper found by John Good at 8:10 p.m., which contains a killer's note: "May they rest in peace, 121, 238, 317." Despite the note, no witnesses come forward.

Later, the narrator and Polo retreat to a "secret lounge" behind the restaurant. Polo expresses anxiety regarding news of a cufflink with the initials "PIJ" found in Jenny's mouth. They discuss the inconsistencies regarding the murder victims. Rafael Bacchus had sent three teacups to Ada Gransbury's room, yet only two were found there; the third was found in Harriet Sippler's room with her body. Poirot questions the logistics of how the teacups were moved and notes that Richard Nigg was found in his room with a glass of sherry that no one had sent him, suggesting the murderer's methods were highly unusual.
